title: pelargonium exstipulatum
intro: Pelargonium exstipulatum, also known as the Cape Geranium or Barberton Geranium, is a species of flowering plant in the family Geraniaceae. It is native to South Africa and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Pelargonium exstipulatum include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. The plant is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule for its medicinal uses, and it is also used in aromatherapy for its calming and relaxing effects.
